diffCoverage.notes: Checked that the refactor preserves the credential-never-in-UI boundary, that the new throttle is on the send path, and that the live smoke sidecar carries no secrets/PII.

resolvedFindings.resolution: A per-account SendThrottle (DEFAULT_SEND_INTERVAL=800ms) now spaces outbound messages per account on the SessionManager::send_text path, reducing the spam-like-burst ban risk (project risk r1). Different accounts are throttled independently; removal clears pacing state.

findings.evidence: The refactor preserves the trust boundary: credentials are still consumed by zalo::login inside the core and never returned across IPC; SessionManager stores only the authenticated Arc<API> handle and the listener, not the credential triple. send_message still validates thread_id/text at the boundary before delegating to the throttled SessionManager::send_text. SessionError messages contain only an account id (a public uid), never token values.

The attested live-smoke sidecar prints only non-secret diagnostics (uid length 19, msg-id length 13) and was scanned for token/recipient leakage — none present. The throttle is a fixed minimum interval, not a hard quota, so it paces but does not block legitimate user-initiated sends.
findings.fix: If automated/bulk sending is added later, layer an explicit per-window quota on top of the interval throttle.

summary: Security review of the session refactor: the credential-never-in-webview boundary is preserved (manager holds only the API handle), boundary input validation is intact, and the new per-account send throttle resolves the previously-accepted ban-risk follow-up (r1 / send-throttle-missing). Live-smoke evidence is free of secrets and PII. No blocking findings.
